questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Polymers are high molecular mass compounds which are formed due to the repetition of the same or similar units.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This repetition of units is responsible for their unique properties.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the repetition of units in polymers is the primary reason for their unique properties, such as high strength and resistance.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Addition polymers are formed by the addition of unsaturated monomers.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This addition reaction involves the opening of the double bond in the monomer.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the opening of the double bond in the monomer is the key step in the formation of addition polymers.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Condensation polymers are formed by the condensation reaction between two or more monomers.
questions.reason: Reason (R): In this reaction, a small molecule such as water is eliminated during the formation of the polymer.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the elimination of a small molecule such as water is a characteristic feature of condensation polymerization.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Polymers can be classified into addition and condensation polymers.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This classification is based on the type of reaction involved in their formation.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the classification of polymers into addition and condensation polymers is indeed based on the type of reaction involved in their formation.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Polymers are generally weak and brittle in nature.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is because they have a high molecular mass and a long chain structure.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because polymers are generally strong and resistant to deformation, not weak and brittle.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Synthetic polymers are man-made polymers.
questions.reason: Reason (R): They are produced by chemical reactions involving monomers.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because synthetic polymers are indeed man-made and are produced by chemical reactions involving monomers.

questions.reason: Reason (R): They can be broken down by microorganisms into simpler substances.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because natural polymers such as cellulose and starch are indeed biodegradable and can be broken down by microorganisms.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Polymers have a high degree of crystallinity.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is because their molecular chains are highly ordered and aligned.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because most polymers have a low degree of crystallinity due to their disordered and randomly packed molecular chains.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Polymers can be modified by chemical reactions.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This modification can alter their physical and chemical properties.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because polymers can indeed be modified by chemical reactions, which can alter their physical and chemical properties.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Polymers are non-reactive in nature.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is because their molecular chains are highly ordered and aligned.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because polymers are not non-reactive in nature, they can undergo various chemical reactions depending on their structure and properties.
